title: Seed default agent_presets (Claude/Codex/Agy) in both default-config generators
action: Seed the exact same 3-entry agent_presets array into both default-config JSON generators: ensure_config's default_json template (src/config/mod.rs, the `if !path.exists()` branch, ~line 846) and doctor's default_config_json (src/doctor/checks.rs, ~line 785) — per D1/D2, both stay content-identical. Presets, in order (D8), Title-Case labels (D9): Claude -> argv ["claude","--dangerously-skip-permissions"] (D5); Codex -> argv ["codex","--sandbox","danger-full-access","--ask-for-approval","never"] (D10, supersedes D6 - do not use --full-auto, it is deprecated); Agy -> argv ["agy","--dangerously-skip-permissions"] (D7). Do not touch AgentPreset/RawAgentPreset shape, src/doctor/edit.rs, or add any PATH/binary-existence probing (D3, D4). Add one test next to each generator asserting the freshly-created/generated document parses (via Config::load_str) to exactly this agent_presets content in this exact order. Confirm (do not need to modify) the existing ensure_config_creates_a_working_default_then_reloads test's second-call-loads-unchanged assertion still passes untouched, proving an existing config.json is never rewritten.

truths: A fresh ensure_config() call on a non-existent config.json path produces a Config whose agent_presets is exactly [Claude/[claude,--dangerously-skip-permissions], Codex/[codex,--sandbox,danger-full-access,--ask-for-approval,never], Agy/[agy,--dangerously-skip-permissions]] in that order.
doctor::checks::default_config_json(home), parsed via Config::load_str, yields the identical agent_presets content as ensure_config's template.

Calling ensure_config() a second time against an already-existing config.json (any content) leaves that file's bytes unchanged — the pre-existing regression guard in ensure_config_creates_a_working_default_then_reloads still passes.

artifacts.substantive: ensure_config's default_json template literal includes the 3 seeded agent_presets entries; a new test asserts the exact parsed content
default_config_json's template literal includes the identical 3 seeded agent_presets entries; a new test asserts the exact parsed content

key_links: Both generators' emitted JSON round-trips through Config::load_str without a ConfigError (satisfies existing RawAgentPreset validation: non-empty label, unique labels, non-empty argv, non-empty argv[0]).
prohibitions: No change to the AgentPreset or RawAgentPreset struct shape.
No change to src/doctor/edit.rs (agent_presets editor already supports add/remove/list, per D4).
No PATH or binary-existence probing added anywhere (per D3).
No code path may modify an already-existing config.json's content.

verify: cargo test --quiet --lib -- config:: doctor::checks:: && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings

trace.outcome: Seeded identical 3-entry agent_presets (Claude/Codex/Agy, D5/D7/D10, Title-Case D9, order D8) into both ensure_config's default_json template and doctor::checks::default_config_json; added tests asserting exact parsed content via Config::load_str/load_file in both modules; existing ensure_config_creates_a_working_default_then_reloads regression guard still passes unchanged.
trace.verify_output: 69 tests passed (0 failed) in config:: and doctor::checks:: including new tests ensure_config_seeds_default_agent_presets and default_config_json_seeds_default_agent_presets; cargo fmt --all --check clean; cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean (Finished, no warnings)
trace.verify_command: cargo test --quiet --lib -- config:: doctor::checks:: && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
